user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Coho Winery.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 13:32 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery consumption trend

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 12:18 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery consumption trend

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 17:05 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:33:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:35:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:40:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Coho Winery: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
